flags.concern: Effect metadata: the plan does not implement the user-requested generated descriptions end-to-end. It adds no `description` field to `ParameterDefinition`, does not ask the edge function for an effect-level description, and does not replace the current save path that persists `description` from the mutable prompt textbox.
flags.evidence:
Two changes addressing the flagged gaps:

1. **Description flow (flag: effect-metadata-description)**: Added `description` field to `ParameterDefinition` type. Updated LLM prompt (Step 4) to generate both a `// DESCRIPTION:` line (well-written effect description) and per-parameter `description` fields. Updated `extractEffectCodeAndMeta()` to parse the description. Updated EffectCreatorPanel (Step 6) to store `generatedDescription` in state, use it instead of `prompt.trim()` when saving metadata, and display it in the UI. Kept a fallback to `prompt.trim()` if the LLM doesn't produce a description.

2. **Default params at all apply sites (flag: clip-effect-state-defaults)**: Expanded Step 10 into two sub-sections (10a/10b) that enumerate all four call sites in ClipPanel that write effect objects: three dropdown `onValueChange` handlers (~lines 135, 172, 209) and the `onSaved` callback (~line 260). Each now looks up the resource's schema and includes `getDefaultValues()` in the written params. Added Step 11 covering the three equivalent call sites in BulkClipPanel (~lines 251, 280, 307). Updated `onSaved` callback signature to pass `defaultParams` from EffectCreatorPanel.

flags.concern: Clip effect state: the plan says per-clip params live in `clip.entrance.params` / `exit.params` / `continuous.params`, but it does not cover how default values get written when a user selects or creates a parameterized effect. In the current repo, all effect-apply paths only write `{type,duration}` or `{type,intensity}`.
flags.evidence:
Two changes addressing the flagged gaps:

1. **Description flow (flag: effect-metadata-description)**: Added `description` field to `ParameterDefinition` type. Updated LLM prompt (Step 4) to generate both a `// DESCRIPTION:` line (well-written effect description) and per-parameter `description` fields. 2. **Default params at all apply sites (flag: clip-effect-state-defaults)**: Expanded Step 10 into two sub-sections (10a/10b) that enumerate all four call sites in ClipPanel that write effect objects: three dropdown `onValueChange` handlers (~lines 135, 172, 209) and the `onSaved` callback (~line 260). flags.concern: Properties panel: BulkClipPanel still omits `ParameterControls`, so parameterized custom effects cannot actually be tuned while multi-select is active.
flags.evidence: The repository routes multi-selection to [PropertiesPanel.tsx](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/PropertiesPanel/PropertiesPanel.tsx#L121), and the bulk branch already exposes effect selectors in [BulkClipPanel.tsx](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/PropertiesPanel/BulkClipPanel.tsx#L246). The revised plan only writes default params in bulk mode and explicitly says not to render `ParameterControls` there, so the existing bulk properties surface still cannot edit parameter values.

flags.concern: Effect schema evolution: the plan says existing clip params will be merged when an effect schema changes, but it never assigns that merge to a real load/render/save path. In this repo, clip effect state is loaded and saved as raw JSON and the render path forwards it verbatim, so clips with stale or partial params will not automatically pick up newly added defaults.
flags.evidence: `ClipMeta` is a plain pass-through container for `entrance`/`exit`/`continuous` ([timeline-data.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-data.ts#L26)); existing clip effect objects are copied in and back out unchanged ([timeline-data.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-data.ts#L106), [timeline-data.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-data.ts#L243)); and `wrapWithClipEffects` just forwards the clip config to the effect component ([effects/index.tsx](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/effects/index.tsx#L109)). The updated plan fixes default-writing at selection time, but it still does not define how already-applied clips are normalized after a schema edit.
